facts.text: Lorenz Park sits on the Hudson River’s western bank, with Claverack Creek to the east, giving it a distinctive riverine setting.
Early settlers included the Mahican people, who used the Hudson Valley river for trade, fishing, and transportation long before Europeans arrived.
Van Dusens and Bunts built stone and brick homes around 1725–1730 along Claverack Creek and South Bay, reflecting Dutch and Palatine German influence.
The New York Central Railroad’s Hudson River line once linked Lorenz Park to regional industrial networks, moving materials and goods across the state.
Route 9 cuts through Lorenz Park, heading south into Hudson and north toward Stockport and Kinderhook.
Lorenz Park lies at 42°15′49″N 73°46′6″W, roughly 27 miles south of Albany, placing it within easy reach of the state capital.
